title: Vanadium. Part IV. Vanadium(IV) and Vanadium(V) Complexes of 3-Hydroxy-I,3 Diphenyltriazene
author: R. L. Dutta
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A study of vanadium (IV) and vanadium (V) complexes of 3-hydroxy-1,3-diphenyltriassene has been made. The reagent reduces vanadium (V) to vanadium (IV) and then gives rise to an olive-green complex [VOT 2 ]. A product of the same composition has also been obtained by interaction of VOSO 4  and the reagent. The identical nature of them two preparations has been established through their X-ray powder patterns, IR spectra, magnetic moment, and molecular weight determinations. On reacting with pyridine, the compound [VOT 2 ] undergoes oxidation and changes to golden yellow [VO 2 . T.py] which has further been found to yield a greenish black isomer from benzene (TH=a molecule of the ligand).
